title: Getting started with vrpr
text: capacitated VRP (CVRP) base case: clients demand, vehicles capacity, minimise total distance. data boundary tibble. Inspect result cost(), routes() (tidy long table) summary(): ggplot2 installed, plot() draws routes:
code: set.seed(1) clients <- tibble::tibble(   x = round(runif(20, -50, 50)),   y = round(runif(20, -50, 50)),   demand = sample(5:15, 20, replace = TRUE) )  model <- vrp_model() |>   add_depot(x = 0, y = 0) |>   add_clients(clients) |>   add_vehicle_type(num_available = 5, capacity = 50)  res <- vrp_solve(model, stop = max_iterations(500), seed = 1, display = FALSE) res #>  #> ── vrpr result ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • cost 567 - feasible #> • 5 routes - 20 clients #> • 500 iterations - 0.19s cost(res) #> [1] 567 head(routes(res)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   route_id depot position client vehicle_type start_service  wait #>      <int> <int>    <int>  <int>        <int>         <dbl> <dbl> #> 1        1     1        1     11            1            29     0 #> 2        1     1        2     12            1            41     0 #> 3        1     1        3      1            1            75     0 #> 4        1     1        4     19            1            99     0 #> 5        2     1        1     14            1            33     0 #> 6        2     1        2      2            1            35     0 summary(res) #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>    cost is_feasible num_routes num_trips num_clients distance iterations runtime #>   <dbl> <lgl>            <int>     <int>       <int>    <dbl>      <int>   <dbl> #> 1   567 TRUE                 5         5          20      567        500   0.193 plot(res)

title: Getting started with vrpr
text: vrp_solve() runs stopping criterion fires. Combine time- iteration-based limits needed:
code: vrp_solve(model, stop = max_runtime(seconds = 10)) # wall-clock budget vrp_solve(model, stop = max_iterations(5000))      # iteration budget vrp_solve(model, stop = no_improvement(1000))      # stop when stuck

title: Getting started with vrpr
text: Add tw_early, tw_late service columns clients turn model VRP time windows. solver respects windows, routes() reports start_service wait time visit.
code: tw_clients <- tibble::tibble(   x        = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),   y        = 0,   demand   = 10,   tw_early = c(0, 30, 60, 90, 120, 150),   tw_late  = c(50, 80, 110, 140, 170, 200),   service  = 10 )  vrptw <- vrp_model() |>   add_depot(0, 0, tw_early = 0, tw_late = 500) |>   add_clients(tw_clients) |>   add_vehicle_type(num_available = 2, capacity = 60, tw_early = 0, tw_late = 500)  res_tw <- vrp_solve(vrptw, stop = max_iterations(500), seed = 1, display = FALSE) routes(res_tw)[, c("route_id", "client", "start_service", "wait")] #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   route_id client start_service  wait #>      <int>  <int>         <dbl> <dbl> #> 1        1      1            50     0 #> 2        1      2            70     0 #> 3        1      3            90     0 #> 4        1      4           110     0 #> 5        1      5           130     0 #> 6        1      6           150     0

title: Getting started with vrpr
text: Call add_vehicle_type() several times fleet different vehicles. cheap type expensive one share capacity; solver prefers cheaper type uses needs.
code: het <- vrp_model() |>   add_depot(0, 0) |>   add_clients(clients) |>   add_vehicle_type(num_available = 3, capacity = 50, unit_distance_cost = 1) |>   add_vehicle_type(num_available = 3, capacity = 50, unit_distance_cost = 5)  res_het <- vrp_solve(het, stop = max_iterations(500), seed = 1, display = FALSE) table(routes(res_het)$vehicle_type) #>  #>  1  2  #> 13  7

title: Getting started with vrpr
text: Add several depots base vehicle type one add_vehicle_type(depot = ). routes() output gains depot column.
code: mdvrp <- vrp_model() |>   add_depot(x = -50, y = 0) |>   add_depot(x =  50, y = 0) |>   add_clients(tibble::tibble(     x = c(-55, -45, -50, 55, 45, 50),     y = c(5, -5, 10, 5, -5, 8),     demand = 10   )) |>   add_vehicle_type(num_available = 3, capacity = 50, depot = 1) |>   add_vehicle_type(num_available = 3, capacity = 50, depot = 2)  res_md <- vrp_solve(mdvrp, stop = max_iterations(500), seed = 1, display = FALSE) routes(res_md)[, c("route_id", "depot", "client")] #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   route_id depot client #>      <int> <int>  <int> #> 1        1     1      1 #> 2        1     1      3 #> 3        1     1      2 #> 4        2     2      4 #> 5        2     2      6 #> 6        2     2      5

title: Getting started with vrpr
text: Mark clients optional required = FALSE give prize. solver visits optional client prize offsets routing cost; unvisited_clients() lists left . add_client_group() defines mutually exclusive alternatives.
code: pc <- vrp_model() |>   add_depot(0, 0) |>   add_clients(tibble::tibble(     x = c(5, -5, 0, 100, 100),     y = c(5, -5, 8, 10, -10),     demand = 10,     required = c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE),     prize = c(0, 0, 0, 5, 500)   )) |>   add_vehicle_type(num_available = 4, capacity = 50)  res_pc <- vrp_solve(pc, stop = max_iterations(500), seed = 1, display = FALSE) unvisited_clients(res_pc) #> [1] 4

title: Getting started with vrpr
text: read_vrplib() read_solomon() read CVRP/VRPTW instances standard VRPLIB/TSPLIB Solomon formats, returning vrpr_model ready solve.
code: path <- system.file("extdata", "sample-n6-k2.vrp", package = "vrpr") read_vrplib(path) |>   vrp_solve(stop = max_iterations(200), seed = 1, display = FALSE) |>   cost() #> ✔ Read "sample-n6-k2": 5 clients, 1 depot, capacity 30, 2 vehicles. #> [1] 68

title: Getting started with vrpr
text: data boundary supports variants: Pickup & delivery / backhaul – add pickup column clients; collected load counts toward capacity along route. Multi-trip – add_vehicle_type(reload_depots = , max_reloads = k) lets vehicle return depot reload run several trips. See ?add_vehicle_type ?add_clients full set options.

what: Vehicle Routing Problem Solver (PyVRP for R)
title: Vehicle Routing Problem Solver (PyVRP for R)
text: PyVRP R — production-grade vehicle routing problem (VRP) solver. vrpr ports PyVRP solver (high-performance, MIT-licensed C++ core) idiomatic R package tidyverse style: pipe-friendly API, data tibbles, logging via {cli}, C++ core rewired {cpp11} — Python runtime dependency. fills real gap R ecosystem: ompr, ROI, lpSolve, igraph dodgr, modern, strong, friendly solver production VRP.

title: Vehicle Routing Problem Solver (PyVRP for R)
text: 🚧 Early development — PyVRP variants already solve end--end: CVRP, VRPTW (time windows), heterogeneous fleet, MDVRP (multiple depots), prize-collecting (optional clients + groups), simultaneous pickup delivery / backhaul, multi-trip. Next: vignettes, CI, CRAN. Parity PyVRP verified (see tools/benchmark/): objective identical bit bit , X-n101-k25 instance (optimum 27591), reach optimum 10 s.
code: library(vrpr)  clients <- tibble::tibble(   x = c(10, 25, 40, 15), y = c(5, 30, 12, 22),   demand = c(10, 15, 8, 12) )  res <- vrp_model() |>   add_depot(x = 0, y = 0) |>   add_clients(clients) |>   add_vehicle_type(num_available = 3, capacity = 50) |>   vrp_solve(stop = max_runtime(2))  cost(res)      # objective cost routes(res)    # tibble: route_id, position, client, vehicle_type summary(res)   # one-row summary

title: Vehicle Routing Problem Solver (PyVRP for R)
text: VRPTW: add tw_early, tw_late, service clients; routes() reports start_service wait per visit. Heterogeneous fleet: call add_vehicle_type() several times different capacities, costs (fixed_cost, unit_distance_cost) shifts. MDVRP: call add_depot() several times use add_vehicle_type(depot = ) base vehicle type depot; routes() reports depot column. Prize-collecting: mark clients required = FALSE prize; solver decides visit. add_client_group() defines mutually exclusive alternatives. See skipped ones unvisited_clients(). Pickup & delivery / backhaul: add pickup column clients (collected load counts toward capacity along route). Multi-trip: add_vehicle_type(reload_depots = , max_reloads = k) lets vehicle return depot reload make several trips (summary()$num_trips).

title: Vehicle Routing Problem Solver (PyVRP for R)
text: read_vrplib() reads CVRP/VRPTW VRPLIB/TSPLIB format (EUC_2D); read_solomon() reads VRPTW Solomon format. return vrpr_model.
code: m <- read_vrplib(system.file("extdata", "sample-n6-k2.vrp", package = "vrpr")) m <- read_solomon(system.file("extdata", "sample-solomon.txt", package = "vrpr")) res <- m |> vrp_solve(stop = max_runtime(5))

title: Vehicle Routing Problem Solver (PyVRP for R)
text: plot() (result model) draws depots (squares), clients (points; unvisited optional ones appear hollow) coloured routes. Requires ggplot2.
code: plot(res)   # depots, clients and routes (one colour per route) with {ggplot2}

title: Vehicle Routing Problem Solver (PyVRP for R)
text: Iterated Local Search (ILS) — inherited PyVRP v0.13.x.

title: Vehicle Routing Problem Solver (PyVRP for R)
text: MIT. Derived PyVRP (© PyVRP authors), whose copyright preserved (see inst/COPYRIGHTS).

what: Add a mutually exclusive group of clients — add_client_group
title: Add a mutually exclusive group of clients — add_client_group
text: Defines group one client visited (exactly one required = TRUE). Useful prize-collecting exclusive alternatives (e.g. serving one several equivalent points). Clients group automatically become optional (individual required = FALSE); use prize encourage visit.

title: Add a mutually exclusive group of clients — add_client_group
code: add_client_group(model, clients, required = FALSE)

title: Add a mutually exclusive group of clients — add_client_group
text: model vrpr_model. clients Vector client numbers (1-based, order add_clients()) form group. required TRUE, exactly one client group must visited; FALSE (default), one.

title: Add a mutually exclusive group of clients — add_client_group
text: updated vrpr_model.

what: Add clients to the model — add_clients
title: Add clients to the model — add_clients
text: Add clients model

title: Add clients to the model — add_clients
code: add_clients(model, data)

title: Add clients to the model — add_clients
text: model vrpr_model. data tibble/data.frame least columns x y. Optional columns: demand (delivery), pickup, tw_early, tw_late, service, release_time, prize, required. time-window columns (tw_early/tw_late/service) enable VRPTW; pickup enables simultaneous pickup delivery / backhaul.

title: Add clients to the model — add_clients
text: updated vrpr_model.

what: Add a depot to the model — add_depot
title: Add a depot to the model — add_depot
text: Add depot model

title: Add a depot to the model — add_depot
code: add_depot(model, x, y, tw_early = 0, tw_late = Inf, service = 0)

title: Add a depot to the model — add_depot
text: model vrpr_model. x, y Depot coordinates. tw_early, tw_late Depot time window (opening/closing). tw_late = Inf leaves closing time unconstrained. service Service time depot (e.g. loading), per trip.

title: Add a depot to the model — add_depot
text: updated vrpr_model.

what: Add a vehicle type to the model — add_vehicle_type
title: Add a vehicle type to the model — add_vehicle_type
text: Add vehicle type model

title: Add a vehicle type to the model — add_vehicle_type
code: add_vehicle_type(   model,   num_available,   capacity,   fixed_cost = 0,   tw_early = 0,   tw_late = Inf,   max_duration = Inf,   unit_distance_cost = 1,   unit_duration_cost = 0,   depot = 1L,   start_depot = depot,   end_depot = depot,   reload_depots = integer(0),   max_reloads = Inf )

title: Add a vehicle type to the model — add_vehicle_type
text: model vrpr_model. num_available Number vehicles available type. capacity Vehicle capacity. fixed_cost Fixed cost per vehicle used. tw_early, tw_late Vehicle shift time window (start/end). tw_late = Inf leaves end shift unconstrained. max_duration Maximum route duration. Inf = unconstrained. unit_distance_cost, unit_duration_cost Variable cost per unit distance duration type. Varying (capacity, fixed_cost) across calls enables heterogeneous fleet. depot Index (1-based) depot vehicles type start return . Shortcut set start_depot end_depot together. start_depot, end_depot Indices (1-based) start end depots, order add_depot(). Varying across types enables MDVRP (multiple depots). reload_depots Indices (1-based) depots vehicles type may reload/empty mid-route, enabling multi-trip routes. Empty (default) = reloading. max_reloads Maximum number reloads per route. Inf = unconstrained.

title: Add a vehicle type to the model — add_vehicle_type
text: updated vrpr_model.

title: Add a vehicle type to the model — add_vehicle_type
text: Call add_vehicle_type() several times fleet multiple vehicle types (different capacities, costs, shifts depots).

title: ILS solver parameters — ils_params
text: ILS solver parameters

title: ILS solver parameters — ils_params
code: ils_params(   num_neighbours = 20L,   min_perturbations = 1L,   max_perturbations = 25L,   init_load = 20,   init_tw = 6,   init_dist = 6,   history_length = 300L,   num_iters_no_improvement = 150000L,   exhaustive_on_best = TRUE )

title: ILS solver parameters — ils_params
text: num_neighbours Granular neighbourhood size (k neighbours per client). min_perturbations, max_perturbations Range perturbations per iteration. init_load, init_tw, init_dist Initial penalties. history_length Length late-acceptance history (> 0). Default 300, PyVRP. num_iters_no_improvement Iterations without improvement restarting best. exhaustive_on_best Refine new best exhaustive search?

title: ILS solver parameters — ils_params
text: list parameters.

what: Read a VRPTW instance in Solomon format — read_solomon
title: Read a VRPTW instance in Solomon format — read_solomon
text: Reads VRPTW instances Solomon (Gehring-Homberger) format, VEHICLE section (number capacity) CUSTOMER table (coordinates, demand, time window service time). Customer 0 depot.

title: Read a VRPTW instance in Solomon format — read_solomon
code: read_solomon(path, num_vehicles = NULL)

title: Read a VRPTW instance in Solomon format — read_solomon
text: path Path file. num_vehicles Number vehicles; NULL, uses value file.

title: Read a VRPTW instance in Solomon format — read_solomon
text: vrp_model() ready vrp_solve().

what: Read an instance in VRPLIB / TSPLIB format — read_vrplib
title: Read an instance in VRPLIB / TSPLIB format — read_vrplib
text: Reads CVRP (VRPTW) instances VRPLIB/CVRPLIB format (extended TSPLIB), X set Uchoa et al. Supports Euclidean coordinates (EDGE_WEIGHT_TYPE : EUC_2D); time-window service-time sections read present.

title: Read an instance in VRPLIB / TSPLIB format — read_vrplib
code: read_vrplib(path, num_vehicles = NULL)

title: Read an instance in VRPLIB / TSPLIB format — read_vrplib
text: path Path .vrp file. num_vehicles Number available vehicles. NULL, uses VEHICLES/TRUCKS field, -k<n> suffix name, – last resort – number clients (always feasible).

title: Read an instance in VRPLIB / TSPLIB format — read_vrplib
text: vrp_model() ready vrp_solve().

title: Routes of a solution, in long (tidy) format — routes
text: tibble one row per visit: route_id, depot (start depot, 1-based), position, client, vehicle_type, start_service (start service) wait (waiting time). last two meaningful time windows (VRPTW); depot varies MDVRP.

title: Cost of a solution — solution_cost
text: penalised cost (numeric scalar). feasible solutions objective cost; feasible attribute reports feasibility.

code: unvisited_clients(x, ...)

text: x vrp_solve() result. ... Unused.

title: Unvisited optional clients — unvisited_clients
text: integer vector (1-based) client numbers visited.

title: Cost evaluator (CostEvaluator) — vrp_cost_evaluator
text: Creates penalised-cost evaluator. Penalties multiply constraint violations (load, time window, maximum distance) form smoothed cost solver minimises. feasible solution, penalised cost equals objective cost.

title: Cost evaluator (CostEvaluator) — vrp_cost_evaluator
code: vrp_cost_evaluator(load_penalties = 1, tw_penalty = 1, dist_penalty = 1)

title: Cost evaluator (CostEvaluator) — vrp_cost_evaluator
text: load_penalties Penalty per unit excess load, per load dimension. scalar recycled across dimensions. tw_penalty Penalty per unit time warp (time-window violation). dist_penalty Penalty per unit distance maximum.

title: Cost evaluator (CostEvaluator) — vrp_cost_evaluator
text: vrpr_cost_evaluator object.

what: Build a vehicle routing (VRP) model — vrp_model

code: vrp_model()

code: clients <- tibble::tibble(   x = c(10, 25, 40), y = c(5, 30, 12),   demand = c(10, 15, 8) ) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ m <- vrp_model() |>   add_depot(x = 0, y = 0) |>   add_clients(clients) |>   add_vehicle_type(num_available = 5, capacity = 100) } # }

what: Assemble the problem data (ProblemData) from a model — vrp_problem_data
title: Assemble the problem data (ProblemData) from a model — vrp_problem_data
text: Builds PyVRP's C++ ProblemData structure vrp_model(). Locations follow PyVRP's convention: depots first (low indices), clients.

title: Assemble the problem data (ProblemData) from a model — vrp_problem_data
code: vrp_problem_data(model, distance = NULL, duration = NULL)

title: Assemble the problem data (ProblemData) from a model — vrp_problem_data
text: model vrp_model() least one depot one vehicle type. distance, duration Matrices (numeric, n x n, locations depots--clients order) distance duration. NULL, computed rounded Euclidean distance coordinates; duration defaults distance.

title: Assemble the problem data (ProblemData) from a model — vrp_problem_data
text: vrpr_problem_data object (wrapper around C++ external pointer).

title: Assemble the problem data (ProblemData) from a model — vrp_problem_data
text: Integer measures (distance, duration, cost, load) travel R numeric integer semantics; non-integer values rejected C++ boundary. Use Inf "unconstrained" limits (e.g. tw_late).

title: Solve a VRP model — vrp_solve
text: Runs iterated local search (ILS) solver model, using PyVRP's vendored C++ core.

title: Solve a VRP model — vrp_solve
code: vrp_solve(model, stop, seed = 42L, params = ils_params(), display = TRUE)

title: Solve a VRP model — vrp_solve
text: model vrp_model() already-assembled vrp_problem_data(). stop stopping criterion (see vrpr_stop), e.g. max_runtime(10). seed Integer seed reproducibility. params Solver parameters (see ils_params()). display Show progress via {cli}?

title: Solve a VRP model — vrp_solve
text: vrpr_result object best solution, cost, routes run statistics. Use cost(), routes() summary() inspect .

title: Solve a VRP model — vrp_solve
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ clients <- tibble::tibble(   x = c(10, 25, 40, 15), y = c(5, 30, 12, 22),   demand = c(10, 15, 8, 12) ) res <- vrp_model() |>   add_depot(x = 0, y = 0) |>   add_clients(clients) |>   add_vehicle_type(num_available = 3, capacity = 50) |>   vrp_solve(stop = max_runtime(2))  cost(res) routes(res) } # }

what: vrpr: Vehicle Routing Problem Solver (PyVRP for R) — vrpr-package
title: vrpr: Vehicle Routing Problem Solver (PyVRP for R) — vrpr-package
text: high-performance, tidyverse-style interface vehicle routing problem (VRP) solving. Ports PyVRP C++ core R via cpp11, Python runtime dependency. Supports capacitated VRP, time windows, multiple depots, heterogeneous fleets prize-collecting variants, driven iterated local search metaheuristic.

title: vrpr: Vehicle Routing Problem Solver (PyVRP for R) — vrpr-package
text: Maintainer: Andre Leite leite@castlab.org Authors: Andre Leite leite@castlab.org Marcos Wasilew marcos.wasilew@gmail.com Hugo Vasconcelos hugo.vasconcelos@ufpe.br Carlos Amorin carlos.agaf@ufpe.br Diogo Bezerra diogo.bezerra@ufpe.br contributors: Niels Wouda (author vendored PyVRP C++ core) [contributor, copyright holder] Thibaut Vidal (HGS-CVRP, parts vendored C++ core) [copyright holder] ORTEC (HGS-DIMACS, parts vendored C++ core) [copyright holder]

title: Solver stopping criteria — vrpr_stop
text: Control iterated local search loop terminate. function returns callable object (closure) solver invokes every iteration, receiving cost current best solution returning TRUE stop.

title: Solver stopping criteria — vrpr_stop
code: max_runtime(seconds)  max_iterations(max_iters)  no_improvement(n)  first_feasible()

title: Solver stopping criteria — vrpr_stop
text: seconds Maximum run time, seconds. max_iters Maximum number iterations. n Number consecutive iterations without improvement stopping.

title: Solver stopping criteria — vrpr_stop
text: object class vrpr_stop: function function(best_cost, feasible) returning TRUE/FALSE.

title: Solver stopping criteria — vrpr_stop
text: R equivalent PyVRP's pyvrp.stop module.
